content.cn_text: 4. 您将听到关于瑞典电影导演英格玛·伯格曼讲座的一部分内容，该讲座由
content.en_text: 4. You will hear part of a lecture on the Swedish film director, Ingmar Bergman, given

content.cn_text: 一位影评人面向媒体研究学生进行。首先，您将有一些时间查看问题
content.en_text: to media study students by a film critic. First, you have some time to look at questions

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细聆听并回答问题31至40。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 你可以阅读一部伟大的小说，聆听一部伟大的交响乐，或者去欣赏一幅伟大
content.en_text: You can read a great novel, listen to a great symphony, or go to see a painting by a great

content.cn_text: 艺术家的画作，你会想一遍又一遍地体验它。每一次体验，你都会
content.en_text: artist, and you'll want to experience it again and again. And every time you do, you'll

content.cn_text: 从中学习到新的东西。很少有电影能做到这一点。它们大多只是纯粹的娱乐
content.en_text: learn something new from it. Very few films achieve this. Most of them are mere entertainment

content.cn_text: 很少有电影能被称为艺术品。它们根本不具备艺术品所拥有的深度
content.en_text: and very few of them could be said to be works of art. They simply don't have the depth

content.cn_text: 这意味着你可能只看一次，但不会想再看
content.en_text: that a work of art has, and this means that you may see them once, but you won't want

content.cn_text: 一遍。
content.en_text: to watch them again.

content.cn_text: 为什么如今这么多商业电影的保质期如此有限？一个问题是
content.en_text: Why is it that so many commercial films today have such a limited shelf life? One problem

content.cn_text: 许多电影一旦你看过一次并知道了情节，就完了。
content.en_text: is that for many films, once you've seen them once and you know what happens, that's

content.cn_text: 它们依靠惊喜元素来保持你的兴趣，一旦惊喜消失，
content.en_text: it. They rely on the element of surprise to keep you interested, and once that's lost,

content.cn_text: 那种接下来会发生什么的感觉也随之消失，就什么也不剩了。
content.en_text: that feeling of what's going to happen next, there's nothing left.

content.cn_text: 此外，电影制作的商业性质意味着，电影不是专注于角色的发展，
content.en_text: In addition, the commercial nature of filmmaking means that rather than focusing on the development

content.cn_text: 许多电影只是成功演员的载体，票房
content.en_text: of characters in the film, many films are just becoming vehicles for actors, box office

content.cn_text: 成功的演员每次都扮演同样的角色。此外，许多评论家
content.en_text: successes, who essentially play the same part each time. Then, as well as this, many critics

content.cn_text: 会认为最伟大的黑白电影拥有自己独特的氛围世界，
content.en_text: would argue that the greatest black and white films had their own special world with

content.cn_text: 有其独特的特殊氛围，而如今很难再创造出这种氛围，因为导演
content.en_text: its own special atmosphere, and that nowadays it's harder to create this because the director

content.cn_text: 无法再像以前那样运用光线。
content.en_text: is unable to play with light in the same way.

content.cn_text: 另一个问题是许多导演缺乏将镜头聚焦于重要点的能力。
content.en_text: Another problem is that many directors lack the ability to focus the camera on the important

content.cn_text: 可能有令人印象深刻的视觉画面，但它们对电影的意义没有贡献。
content.en_text: points. There may be impressive visual images, but they don't contribute to the meaning
of the film. 。

content.cn_text: 这让我联想到英格玛·伯格曼，这位伟大的瑞典电影导演于2007年去世。
content.en_text: Which brings me on to Ingmar Bergman, the great Swedish film director who died in 2007.

content.cn_text: 在传达情感的能力上，没有其他导演能与伯格曼媲美。他的电影拥有
content.en_text: No other director has equaled Bergman in his ability to convey emotion. His films have

content.cn_text: 世界上最伟大剧作家（如易卜生或莎士比亚）作品的戏剧张力。
content.en_text: the dramatic force of a work by the world's greatest playwrights such as Ibsen or Shakespeare.

content.cn_text: 观看伯格曼的电影是一种丰富的体验。没有什么是显而易见或可预测的，
content.en_text: Watching Bergman's films is an enriching experience. Nothing is immediately obvious or predictable,

content.cn_text: 但你逐渐会明白他要带你去往何方。
content.en_text: but gradually it becomes clear where he is taking you.

content.cn_text: 如果你研究像《野草莓》这样的电影，你会发现每一个小细节都意义重大。
content.en_text: If you study a film such as Wild Strawberries, you'll see that every little detail is significant.

content.cn_text: 屏幕上的每一个图像都有其目的，并逐渐建立起对
content.en_text: Each image on the screen is there for a purpose, and gradually builds up an understanding

content.cn_text: 中心意义的理解。对于伯格曼的电影，你也知道角色做的一个小手势
content.en_text: of the central meaning. With Bergman too, you know that a small gesture made by a character

content.cn_text: 或角色脸上的表情所传达的信息，可能与说出的话语一样多。
content.en_text: or the expression on a character's face can tell you just as much as the words uttered.

content.cn_text: 在他的电影《假面》中，中心角色一言不发，但意义通过
content.en_text: In his film Persona, the central character says nothing, but meaning is conveyed by

content.cn_text: 我们所看到的屏幕画面传达。
content.en_text: what we see on the screen.

content.cn_text: 但伯格曼不只是一位伟大的电影摄影师，他也是一位伟大的故事讲述者。
content.en_text: But Bergman wasn't just a great cinematographer. He was also a great storyteller.

content.cn_text: 他的每部电影都遵循相似的模式，故事被组织在两个层面。
content.en_text: Each of his films follows a similar pattern in that the story is organized on two levels.

content.cn_text: 首先，是直接的戏剧叙事层面。其次，是
content.en_text: Firstly, on the level of a straightforward, dramatic narrative. But secondly, on the level

content.cn_text: 神话层面，故事在这两个层面贯穿整部电影。
content.en_text: of myth, and the story works throughout the film on both these levels.

content.cn_text: 在伯格曼的电影《野草莓》中，一位老人回到了他小时候和年轻时熟悉的地方。
content.en_text: In Bergman's film Wild Strawberries, an old man travels back to the places he knew as

content.cn_text: 在电影中，我们可以识别出伯格曼作品中反复出现的主题。
content.en_text: a child and a young man. In the film, we can identify themes that occur again and again

content.cn_text: 首先，我们有旅程的理念。部分旅程
content.en_text: in Bergman's work. First of all, we have the idea of the journey. Part of the journey

content.cn_text: 是真实的，但与之相关联的是梦境的主题，梦境能带我们去向何方以及我们能从中学习到什么。
content.en_text: is a real one, but linked with that is the theme of dreams, where they can take us and

content.cn_text: 。另一个贯穿
content.en_text: what we can learn from them. Another theme, which was to be central

content.cn_text: 伯格曼一生作品的核心主题是关系，尤其是
content.en_text: to Bergman's work throughout his life, is that of relationships, and in particular,

content.cn_text: 维系一个家庭成员的纽带。最后，正如《野草莓》中的老人在
content.en_text: the ties that join the members of a family together. And finally, as the old man in Wild

content.cn_text: 挣扎着理解自己的人生时，我们看到了另一个贯穿他所有作品的主题。
content.en_text: Strawberries struggles to make sense of his life, we have another theme which underlies all

content.cn_text: 无论他的角色看起来多么焦虑或不快乐，这个主题就是，
content.en_text: his work. No matter how anxious or unhappy his characters may appear to be, and that

content.cn_text: 我们必须接受生活所给予的一切。这不是一种时髦的哲学，
content.en_text: is, that we have to accept everything that life can give. It's not a fashionable philosophy,

content.cn_text: 这可能就是伯格曼的电影过时的原因。
content.en_text: and it may be why Bergman's films have fallen out of fashion.

content.cn_text: 第四部分到此结束。您现在有一分钟时间检查您在第四部分的答案。
content.en_text: That is the end of Part 4. You now have one minute to check your answers to Part 4.

groups.desc:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
groups.title: Questions 31-40.

items.title: Most films are just entertainment – they lack the 31. _____ that is necessary for great art.
Character development is regarded as unimportant and they depend on using successful 32. _____ in the films.
Directors are unable to use 33. _____ to create an atmosphere.
He had a unique ability to portray 34. _____
Every image has a 35. _____ and contributes to the overall meaning.
A character's gestures or facial expressions can be as important as his or her 36. _____
His stories work at two levels, as drama and as 37. _____
The need to 40. _____ all that life offers

groups.source_collect:
Complete the notes below.
Write ONE WORD ONLY for each answer.
Ingmar Bergman: Film Director
Introduction
• Most films are just entertainment – they lack the $31$ that is necessary for great art.
Problems with modern commercial films
• They depend too much on surprise for their effect.
• Character development is regarded as unimportant and they depend on using successful $32$ in the films.
• Directors are unable to use $33$ to create an atmosphere.
• Directors lack the ability to focus on what is important.
Dramatic impact of Bergman's films
• He had a unique ability to portray $34$
• Every image has a $35$ and contributes to the overall meaning.
• A character's gestures or facial expressions can be as important as his or her $36$
• His stories work at two levels, as drama and as $37$
Common themes in Wild Strawberries
• The connection between journeys and $38$
• $39$ relationships
• The need to $40$ all that life offers
